'''Nathan Graham''' (born 23 November 1971) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of [[Huddersfield Giants Women]] and a former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|hk}} or {{rlp|lf}} in the 1990s and 2000s.

He played at international level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and at club level for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] (two spells), [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[York City Knights]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Graham played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|fb}} in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final loss to St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> He made 26 appearances for [[York City Knights]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web|title=Appearances 04|url=http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances04.htm|publisher=York City Knights|accessdate=19 May 2015|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20060928194652/http://www.yorkcityknights.co.uk/mainFrame/appearances04.htm|archivedate=28 September 2006}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
Nathan Graham made his début for [[Featherstone Rovers]] on 2 December 2001.

===International===
Graham was a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international and played at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Coaching career==
===Keighley Cougars===
After his playing career ended Graham moved into coaching and in October 2016 joined [[Keighley Cougars]] as assistant coach after five years of amateur team Drighlington ARLFC.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/sportrugbyleague/rugbyleaguecougars/14801334.Former_Bradford_Bulls_Challenge_Cup_finalist_appointed_Keighley_Cougars_assistant|title=Former Bradford Bulls Challenge Cup finalist appointed Keighley Cougars assistant|newspaper=Keighley News|date=13 October 2016|accessdate=8 December 2016}}</ref>

===Scotland===
Graham was appointed head coach of [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at the end of 2019.<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sky Sports|date=21 January 2020|accessdate=8 January 2021|title=Scotland head coach Nathan Graham ready for Rugby League World Cup return|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11914073/scotland-head-coach-nathan-graham-ready-for-rugby-league-world-cup-return}}</ref>

On 1 August 2025 it was reported that he had stepped down from the {{rlnt|Scotland}} head coach role<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=1 Aug 2025|accessdate=2 Aug 2025|author=Doug Thomson|title=Scotland seek new coach after Nathan Graham calls time|url=https://www.totalrl.com/scotland-seek-new-coach-after-nathan-graham-calls-time/}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants Women===
On 6 November 2025 he was announced as the new head coach of [[Huddersfield Giants Women]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=6 Nov 2025|accessdate=7 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Huddersfield Giants appoint new head coach to Women’s Super League side|url=https://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-giants-appoint-new-head-coach-to-womens-super-league-side/}}</ref>
